county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1869 from unorganized territory in northeast Lower Michigan. County seat is Harrisville on Lake Huron. Named for a coinage by Henry Schoolcraft. Sparsely populated lumber and resort county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Alcona County Register of Deeds in Harrisville holds land records from the county's 1869 organization. The County Clerk maintains marriage records and circuit court files. Alcona was sparsely settled lumber country -- researchers should check lumber company records at the Archives of Michigan in Lansing for worker rosters and payroll records. The Alcona County Library and Historical Society have local cemetery transcriptions and newspaper clippings. Early settlers came for the lumber boom (1870s-1890s), and many families left when timber was exhausted. Federal land patents through BLM GLO are essential for tracing original settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed Alcona County vital records and some probate files.

source_profiles.source_name: Alcona County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1869 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Alcona County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1869. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Alcona County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1869. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Alcona County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Alcona County from 1870 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Alcona County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Local historical and genealogical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Alcona County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1885 from Schoolcraft County in the Upper Peninsula. County seat is Munising. Named for Russell A. Alger, Michigan governor and US Secretary of War. Home to Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore.
county_metadata.research_tips: Alger County Register of Deeds in Munising holds land records from 1885 organization. Upper Peninsula county created from Schoolcraft. The Alger County Historical Society in Munising preserves local history materials. Lumber and iron mining drove early settlement -- check mining company records at Michigan Technological University Archives in Houghton and the Archives of Michigan. Finnish and Scandinavian immigrants predominated in the UP; church records from Finnish Lutheran and Swedish congregations are important. Pictured Rocks area attracted early tourism. FamilySearch microfilm includes Alger County vital records. Probate Court in Munising has estate records from 1885.

source_profiles.source_name: Alger County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1835 from unorganized territory in southwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Allegan. Named for a corruption of Algonquin. Agricultural county with Lake Michigan shoreline.
county_metadata.research_tips: Allegan County Register of Deeds holds land records from 1835 when the county was organized. County Clerk in Allegan maintains marriage licenses from 1836 and circuit court records. Probate Court has estate files from 1836. The Allegan County Historical Society and Old Jail Museum preserve local genealogical materials. Dutch settlers (related to Holland colony in neighboring Ottawa County) established communities in the eastern part. Strong Quaker presence in early settlement period. Underground Railroad routes passed through Allegan County -- check the Archives of Michigan for related documentation. FamilySearch has microfilmed vital records and probate files. BLM GLO land patents document original purchasers.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegan County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegan County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1835. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegan County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1835. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Allegan County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegan County Dutch Reformed Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Joint Archives of Holland / Calvin University Heritage Hall / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Reformed Church in America (RCA) and Christian Reformed Church (CRC) records from Dutch immigrant congregations. Baptisms, memberships, marriages, and death records. The Joint Archives of Holland (Hope College) and Calvin University Heritage Hall in Grand Rapids hold extensive Dutch Reformed church records. Essential for tracing Dutch immigrant families who settled in western Michigan from 1847.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegan County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1835 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.source_name: Allegan County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Allegan County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1857 from unorganized territory in northeast Lower Michigan. County seat is Alpena on Thunder Bay (Lake Huron). Named for a Schoolcraft coinage. Major lumber center in the 19th century.
county_metadata.research_tips: Alpena County Register of Deeds holds land records from 1857 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage records and court files. The Jesse Besser Museum in Alpena has local history and genealogy collections including early newspapers. Alpena was a major lumber center on Lake Huron -- Thunder Bay lumber operations records are valuable. German and Polish immigrants formed significant communities; check St. Mary's and St. John's Catholic parish records for baptisms, marriages, and burials. The Alpena County Library has a genealogy collection. Federal census records (1860-1950) show rapid population growth during the lumber boom. FamilySearch microfilm covers Alpena County vital records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1863 from unorganized territory in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Bellaire. Named after County Antrim, Ireland. Scenic county with Torch Lake and Elk Rapids.
county_metadata.research_tips: Antrim County Register of Deeds in Bellaire holds land records from 1863. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and circuit court records. The county was settled primarily by New England and New York migrants, with some Irish and Scottish families (the county is named after County Antrim in Ireland). Lumber was the primary industry. The Antrim County Historical Museum and local genealogical society have compiled cemetery records and family files. Elk Rapids and Bellaire newspapers document local events. BLM GLO records show federal land patents. FamilySearch has microfilmed vital records. Check the Archives of Michigan for territorial and early statehood records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1883 from Bay County. County seat is Standish. Named from a combination of Latin 'arena' (sandy) and Algonquin suffix. Located along Saginaw Bay.
county_metadata.research_tips: Arenac County Register of Deeds in Standish holds land records from 1883 when the county was organized from Bay County. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Arenac County Historical Society preserves local records and photographs. Lumber industry drove early settlement along the AuGres and Rifle Rivers. Some French-Canadian families settled along the Saginaw Bay coast. For pre-1883 records, check Bay County since Arenac was part of Bay. FamilySearch has microfilmed vital records. The Archives of Michigan in Lansing holds supplementary records. BLM GLO land patents are essential for original settlers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1875 from Houghton County in the Upper Peninsula. County seat is L'Anse. Named for Bishop Frederic Baraga, Catholic missionary to the Ojibwe. Contains part of Keweenaw Bay Indian Community reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Baraga County Register of Deeds in L'Anse holds land records from 1875. The county was named for Bishop Frederic Baraga, a missionary to the Ojibwe/Chippewa. The L'Anse Reservation (Keweenaw Bay Indian Community) is within Baraga County -- tribal enrollment and census records are held by the tribal government and NARA (Great Lakes Agency records). Finnish immigrants were a major population group; check Finnish Evangelical Lutheran church records. Copper and iron mining records at Michigan Technological University Archives. The Baraga County Historical Museum has local materials. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Probate Court in L'Anse has estate records from 1875.

source_profiles.source_name: Baraga County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1875 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Baraga County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1875. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1875. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Baraga County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Baraga County Mining Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from copper mining operations in Baraga County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

source_profiles.source_name: Ojibwe/Chippewa Tribal Records -- Baraga County Area
source_profiles.repository: NARA (Great Lakes Agency) / Tribal Government Offices / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ojibwe/Chippewa communities in Baraga County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1839 from unorganized territory in southwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Hastings. Named for William T. Barry, US Postmaster General. Agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Barry County Register of Deeds in Hastings holds land records from 1839 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from 1839 and court records. Probate Court has estate files from 1839. The Barry County Historical Society in Hastings has genealogical materials. Early settlers came from New York and New England via the Erie Canal route. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records from the 1840s. Barry County was largely agricultural with some milling. The Thornapple River valley attracted early settlement. FamilySearch has microfilmed vital records and probate files. BLM GLO land patents document first purchasers. Check the Archives of Michigan for territorial period records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1857 from Midland and Arenac in east-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Bay City. Named for Saginaw Bay. Major lumber center; later industrial development.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bay County Register of Deeds in Bay City holds land records from 1857 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. The Bay County Historical Society has extensive genealogical collections. Bay City was a major lumber center -- sawmill records and worker documentation are valuable. German, Polish, and French-Canadian immigrants settled in large numbers; check St. Stanislaus (Polish), Trinity Episcopal, and Catholic parish records. The Bay City Branch Library genealogy room has city directories, newspapers, and family files. Saginaw Valley lumbering records at the Archives of Michigan include Bay County materials. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records and some church records. Federal census records from 1860 onward show the lumber boom population growth.

source_profiles.source_name: Bay County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1857 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1869 from Leelanau County in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Beulah. Named from French 'bec scié' (sawbill duck). Scenic county on Lake Michigan.
county_metadata.research_tips: Benzie County Register of Deeds in Beulah holds land records from 1869 when the county was organized from Leelanau. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Benzie Area Historical Museum in Benzonia preserves local history including photographs and oral histories. Early settlement centered on lumber and fishing on Lake Michigan. Congregational and Presbyterian church records document New England settler families. Grand Traverse area resources at Traverse Area District Library supplement Benzie County research. FamilySearch microfilm includes vital records. BLM GLO land patents for original purchasers. Check Leelanau County for pre-1869 records.
